spec.reasoning: We apply two safe anchor types: lexical renames and a distractor column. For lexical changes: (1) 'Annual Asking Rent (P/SQM)' is renamed to 'Annual Asking Rent (Per SQM)' — same meaning, same descriptiveness, just expanding the abbreviation slightly. (2) 'Gross Potential Rent (GPR)' is renamed to 'Gross Potential Rent (Total)' — same concept, same descriptiveness level, keeping 'Rent' intact since the instruction doesn't use this column name as general English. (3) 'Sheet2' is renamed to 'LOOKUP_TABLE' — the instruction references 'Sheet2' by name, but this is a surface anchor change that tests whether the agent finds the sheet by name or by content. We do NOT rename 'DATA' because the INPUTS sheet has formulas like '=DATA!B4*DATA!B7' that would break if the sheet name changes without updating those references, and the previous attempt failed on this. We do NOT rename 'Advertising & Marketing' or 'Electricity' since these appear as meaningful terms in the instruction text. A distractor column 'Adjustment Factor' is added to the lookup table sheet to test whether the agent incorrectly includes it in range references. No data_value_renames are needed since the renamed columns don't appear as data values elsewhere.
